---
title: "Connecting Survival Analysis and Infectious Disease Modeling"
subtitle: "Lorentz Center Workshop | 15–19 June 2026 | Leiden, the Netherlands"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

## About the Workshop

Survival analysis (SA) and infectious disease modelling (IDM) are two disciplines that frequently tackle similar scientific problems — time-to-event outcomes, heterogeneity in populations, and the effects of interventions — yet they do so with largely separate methodological frameworks. This workshop brings together researchers from both communities to make these (dis)connections explicit, learn from one another, and develop shared tools for cross-disciplinary analysis.

Over five days, participants will work in small mixed groups on a shared simulated epidemic dataset and collectively produce vignettes, code examples, and a "Ten Simple Rules" guide for SA–IDM integration.

## What's on This Site

| Page | What you'll find |
|------|-----------------|
| [Programme](https://www.lorentzcenter.nl/connecting-survival-analysis-and-infectious-disease-modeling.html) | Full daily schedule on the Lorentz Center website |
| [Preparation](preparation.qmd) | Pre-course reading, R software setup, and case study data |
| [Case Study](case-studies.qmd) | Group questions (A–E) and the downloadable case study document |
| [Presentations](assets/presentations/index.qmd) | All workshop slide decks in one place |
| [Vignettes](vignettes/index.qmd) | Cross-disciplinary worked examples produced during the workshop |
| [Resources](vignettes/resources.qmd) | Papers, software, and links shared by participants |
| [Organizers](organizers.qmd) | Scientific organizers and their affiliations |

Day-by-day slides and code, plus the Presentations, Vignettes, and Resources pages, are all available via the **Materials** menu in the navigation bar. Use **Contribute** to find instructions for pushing your group's analysis scripts.

## Scientific Organizers

The workshop is organized by six researchers from the SA and IDM communities. See the [Organizers](organizers.qmd) page for full profiles.

| Name | Affiliation |
|---|---|
| Liesbeth de Wreede | Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC) |
| Hein Putter | Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC) |
| Kylie Ainslie | University of Melbourne & University of Hong Kong |
| Don Klinkenberg | RIVM & Wageningen University and Research |
| Jacco Wallinga | RIVM & Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC) |
| Steven Abrams | UHasselt & University of Antwerp |

## Venue

**[Lorentz Center @lambda](https://www.lorentzcenter.nl)**
Niels Bohrweg 2, 2333 CA Leiden, the Netherlands

This workshop is part of the [NIAS-Lorentz Program](https://www.lorentzcenter.nl/about/nias-lorentz-program.html).

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1rem 0; border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);">
  <iframe
    src="https://www.openstreetmap.org/export/embed.html?bbox=4.459%2C52.163%2C4.481%2C52.173&layer=mapnik&marker=52.1676%2C4.4700"
    width="100%" height="300" style="border:0; display:block;"
    allowfullscreen="" loading="lazy">
  </iframe>
</div>
<p style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#6c757d;">
  <a href="https://www.openstreetmap.org/?mlat=52.1676&mlon=4.4700#map=15/52.1676/4.4700" target="_blank">View larger map</a>
</p>
```

---

## Workshop Poster

::: {.text-center}
[Download Poster (PDF)](images/workshop-poster.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"}
:::

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1.5rem auto; max-width: 700px; border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);">
  <embed src="images/workshop-poster.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="900px" style="display:block;" />
</div>
```
